searchQuery: Achilles tendon force seated standing unilateral weighted calf raise hop external load
populationScope: Healthy adult exercise evidence; condition-specific models only
exerciseConditionScope: Seated, standing, unilateral, and repeated hopping kept distinct
decisionReason: Standing single-leg and repeated unilateral hopping retain narrow anchors; seated LOW and arbitrary weighted scaling are removed.
researchNotes: No HIGH band was invented; external-load dose response remains blocked.

searchQuery: Achilles tendon loading rate heel raise hop protocol
decisionReason: Loading rates exist but do not establish app-wide rate bands and cannot be compared with peak-force units.
researchNotes: Same-condition provenance is preserved without cross-dimension aggregation.

searchQuery: Achilles tendon cyclic load repetitions impulse calf raise hopping
exerciseConditionScope: Repetition count, cadence, and contact duration required
measurementScope: Cyclic exposure distinct from one-repetition peak and source loading index
decisionReason: Peak and exercise-level impulse data do not define a reusable cyclic tensile-load rubric.

searchQuery: Achilles tendon energy storage release hopping jumping rehabilitation
measurementScope: Direct tendon energy storage and release
decisionReason: Force, impulse, and loading rate cannot substitute for directly quantified tendon energy.

searchQuery: patellar tendon force weighted squat front back RFESS split squat lunge external load
exerciseConditionScope: Bodyweight and externally loaded protocols kept separate
measurementScope: Modeled internal patellar tendon peak force
decisionReason: Bodyweight squat and Bulgarian claims are preserved, but weighted back/front squat and loaded unilateral variants cannot be populated.
researchNotes: Weighted PFJ studies do not supply a patellar-tendon force multiplier.

searchQuery: patellar tendon strain squat external load ultrasound biomechanics
decisionReason: Modeled tendon force cannot be relabeled as tendon strain; available strain methods do not establish these exercise rubrics.

searchQuery: patellar tendon cyclic load squat jump landing repetitions
decisionReason: Peak, impulse, and rate rows do not establish cyclic tendon exposure across sessions.
researchNotes: No set or repetition multiplier was invented.

searchQuery: patellar tendon eccentric load squat decline squat lunge biomechanics
exerciseConditionScope: Concentric and eccentric phases must be resolved
decisionReason: Current governed candidates do not support a generic eccentric-load band.
researchNotes: Phase labels cannot be inferred from exercise names.

searchQuery: patellar tendon loading rate squat Bulgarian jump hop rehabilitation
exerciseConditionScope: Exact protocol, speed, and landing required
decisionReason: The source reports condition-specific rates but does not establish generic app intervals or external-load scaling.

searchQuery: patellofemoral peak force weighted squat lunge Bulgarian jump hop
exerciseConditionScope: Load, ROM, lunge direction, and jump protocol kept explicit
decisionReason: Bodyweight source values are preserved; 35 percent BW squat and 12RM lunge evidence confirm load sensitivity but do not define common app bands.
researchNotes: PMID 40705768 was additionally reviewed for 70-90 percent 1RM powerlifting squat context; its population/model were not merged into the governed values.

searchQuery: patellofemoral loading impulse squat lunge jump hop duration
decisionReason: Slow squat and lunge can have greater impulse than faster landing tasks; this is not contradictory to peak/rate ordering.
researchNotes: Composite-index tiers were removed rather than reused.

searchQuery: patellofemoral loading rate squat lunge jump hop landing
exerciseConditionScope: Exact velocity and landing strategy required
decisionReason: Fast jump/hop rates exceed slow squat/lunge rates in the same model, but no app-wide rate intervals are approved.
researchNotes: Anomaly audit compares like metrics only.

searchQuery: patellofemoral impact impulse jump hop landing force plate
exerciseConditionScope: Drop height, direction, landing strategy, and laterality required
measurementScope: PFJ-specific impact impulse distinct from general GRF impact
decisionReason: PFJ compression impulse and ground-reaction impact impulse are not interchangeable.
researchNotes: Blocked rather than inferred from force-plate impact alone.
